headline: Stocks to Watch Thursday: Apple, Exxon, ASML
summary: ↘️ Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), Tesla (TSLA): The three tech stocks were the most heavily traded S&P 500 components before the U.S. bell. All fell, weighing on index futures. ↘️ UniCredit (IT:UCG), Deutsche Bank (XE:DB), Santander (ES:SAN): European bank stocks fell as government bond yields in the region rose, weighing on the price of securities on the lenders' books.

headline: GM to invest over $150m in Saginaw casting facility in US
summary: The automaker said the funding will be used to install new equipment and tooling at the Saginaw facility, forming part of its wider manufacturing plans.

headline: General Motors Resets EV Plans As Valuation Signals Potential Upside
summary: General Motors (NYSE:GM) is restructuring its manufacturing and EV plans, scaling back electric vehicle production at Factory ZERO. The company is temporarily laying off 1,300 workers at the Detroit plant while pausing certain EV operations. At the same time, GM is ramping up output of gas powered trucks and introducing new generative AI driven design processes. These moves reflect a broad operational reset across GM's vehicle mix and product development approach. For investors following...

headline: The 30% Margin Cash Cow Funding Tesla’s Trillion-Dollar AI Bet
summary: Tesla's (TSLA) long-term valuation now rests on two AI bets: the Cybercab robotaxi network and the Optimus humanoid robot. The problem? Both are currently cost centers, and that means the company needs cash. So where does it find it? The answer might surprise you.

content.title: The 30% Margin Cash Cow Funding Tesla’s Trillion-Dollar AI Bet

headline: Ford, GM EV Sales Plummet. What That Means for the Stocks.
summary: Thursday,  Ford Motor  reported first-quarter U.S. sales.  Fourth-quarter sales fell after buyers rushed to beat the September expiration of the $7,500 federal EV purchase tax credit.  First quarter hybrid electric vehicle sales were 51,073, down 20% from a year ago, accounting for 9% of total car sales, down 1 percentage point from a year ago.

content.title: Ford, GM EV Sales Plummet. What That Means for the Stocks.
content.summary: Thursday,  Ford Motor  reported first-quarter U.S. sales.  Fourth-quarter sales fell after buyers rushed to beat the September expiration of the $7,500 federal EV purchase tax credit.  First quarter hybrid electric vehicle sales were 51,073, down 20% from a year ago, accounting for 9% of total car sales, down 1 percentage point from a year ago.
